description: New repo — propose only the instruction documents that are actually needed
input: I'm starting a new Node.js API repo. I'll use GitHub Copilot and sometimes Claude Code. Do I need AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, and GEMINI.md?

description: AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md have duplicated rules — consolidate without adding new documents
input: Our AGENTS.md says 'do not push to main without review' and our CLAUDE.md also says 'always use a PR for main'. They're saying the same thing in two places. How should we fix this?

description: GEMINI.md is optional — should not be recommended when Gemini is not used
input: We only use Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. Someone suggested we add a GEMINI.md. Should we?
